is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study of children with uncomplicated Plasmodium falciparum malaria; PfCRT codon 76 assayed.

reports_target_markers.evidence: Assays for the T76 molecular marker in PfCRT (Pfcrt codon 76), one of the curated positions.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: 'Assays for the T76 molecular marker for chloroquine resistance in PfCRT were successfully performed on 199 of the 210 filter-paper samples... All samples had the wild-type K76 PfCRT genotype' — 0/199 mutant, extractable frequency.

sub_country_location.evidence: All samples collected at Ndirande District Health Centre, a township on the outskirts of Blantyre, Malawi — a specific sub-national site.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: 'From May through November 2005... Follow-up continued through December 2005.' Collection within a single year.

african_field_samples.evidence: Human clinical isolates from children in Blantyre, Malawi (African country), field/clinical samples.

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary randomized clinical trial (ClinicalTrials.gov NCT00125489) with original molecular data.

duplicate_risk.evidence: New field collection from a specific 2005 trial at the Blantyre Malaria Project; related prior surveys (Kublin 2003) exist but this is a distinct new collection.

supplement.evidence: Supplementary Appendix mentioned only for sequencing details; the extractable frequency (0/199 K76 wild-type) is in the main text.

assessment.summary: Randomized clinical trial in Blantyre (Ndirande), Malawi, May–Dec 2005, of chloroquine vs SP in 210 children with P. falciparum malaria. Pfcrt K76T assayed successfully in 199 enrollment samples; all were wild-type K76 (0/199 mutant), giving an extractable frequency tied to a sub-country site and a single-year window. Eligible on all criteria.
